author: Diexar Labs
description: A private, open-source Google Keep alternative: a Google Keep-style quick-capture card grid with a companion Android app and Chrome web clipper. Offline, no account, plain Markdown. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Diexar Labs
manifest.description: A private, offline Google Keep alternative for your vault: a Google Keep–style quick-capture card grid, paired with a companion Android app (share-sheet, OCR, voice) and a Chrome web clipper. No account, no cloud, every note plain Markdown.
manifest.name: JotDrop

name: JotDrop

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description: Metadata-only release: repositions JotDrop as a private, open-source Google Keep alternative across all public descriptions (manifest, README, GitHub) for discoverability. No code or behaviour changes.

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
Clarifies that JotDrop is a trio — the plugin pairs with a companion Android app and a Chrome web clipper for the full Google Keep experience. No functional changes to your notes.

- Manifest description now names the companion Android app + Chrome web clipper
- README reframed as a "plugin + app + clipper" trio with an install-all-three callout
- The card view's empty state nudges new users toward the app + extension (EN/NL)
- Includes the 0.16.1 review-compliance fix

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
JotDrop Android v0.23.0

- **In-app update check**: the app now checks GitHub for new releases (once a day, in the background) and shows a dismissible banner on the dashboard when a newer version is available, with a button to the release page.
- **Manual check**: a "Check for updates" item in the overflow menu (⋮); tells you when you're already on the latest version.
- Colorblind-safe (icon + text, not color), fails silently with no network.

Android-only release; the Obsidian plugin is unchanged (still 0.15.0).

APK: jotdrop-debug.apk (debug-signed).
releases.name: JotDrop Android v0.23.0

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
JotDrop Android v0.22.0

- **Title + body**: the editor now has a separate title field above the body. Leave it empty and the first words of the body become the title automatically.
- **Stable card order**: opening or editing a card no longer pushes it to the top. Cards are ordered by creation time (newest first), pinned on top.
- **Cleaner link cards**: shared links (e.g. TikTok) no longer appear twice on a card.
- **Smoother scrolling**: thumbnail decoding is bounded to card size and list items recycle better, reducing stutter on large grids.

APK: jotdrop-debug.apk (debug-signed).
releases.name: JotDrop Android v0.22.0

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
Review-compliance fix on top of 0.16.0. No functional changes and no change to your notes.

- Stops disabling the `import/no-nodejs-modules` lint rule (the review bot forbids disabling it); it stays a harmless warning, as it was before. The optional desktop clip server still uses Node's `http` exactly as before.

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
Maintenance release to pass the Obsidian community plugin review checks. No new features, and **no changes to your notes' format or sync** — existing notes render identically.

Under the hood:
- Card previews are built as DOM nodes instead of `innerHTML` (safer rendering)
- Inline styles moved to CSS classes; settings headings use the Settings API
- Popout-window-safe timers and document access
- Uses `getLanguage()`, `Notice.messageEl`, and `FileManager.trashFile()` per current API guidance — **deleting a note now respects your "Deleted files" preference**
- Minimum Obsidian version raised to **1.8.7**

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
- **Title + body**: notes now have a dedicated title field above the body. Leave it empty and the first words of the body become the title automatically (unchanged fallback).
- **Stable card order**: opening or editing a card no longer pushes it to the top. Cards are ordered by creation time (newest first), pinned on top. The default sort is now "Created, newest first"; existing installs migrate once.
- **Cleaner link cards**: shared links (e.g. TikTok) no longer appear twice on a card.
- **Performance**: lighter thumbnail rendering when scrolling large grids.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
Kleine UI-fix op het dashboard.

## Wijzigingen
- **Mic-FAB kleur-pariteit:** de voicememo-FAB op het dashboard heeft in idle-state nu dezelfde container-/content-kleur als de foto- en plus-FAB (Material3 `primaryContainer` / `onPrimaryContainer`). Eerder gebruikte hij `secondaryContainer` en viel daardoor in een afwijkende tint uit.
- Tijdens opname blijft de FAB rood (`errorContainer`) met het Stop-icoon — de vormwissel mic ↔ stop is en blijft de primaire feedback (kleur is secundair).

## Niet gewijzigd
Alle voicememo-functionaliteit, audio-player, equalizer-banner en overige flows werken ongewijzigd.

## Install
APK: `jotdrop-debug.apk` hieronder. Eerst de oude versie deïnstalleren als je vanaf v0.20.x of eerder upgrade is niet nodig — versionCode 46 over 45 werkt direct.
releases.name: JotDrop 0.21.2 — mic-FAB kleur-pariteit

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new

Minor patch on top of v0.21.0. No user-facing UI changes — the app's brand remains **JotDrop** (one word). Installs cleanly over v0.21.0 (same package id and debug keystore), no data loss.

## What carries over

This APK includes everything from the JotDrop rebrand (v0.21.0):

- Renamed from ObsiDrop after Obsidian's trademark review.
- All app strings + theme + share-target label now show "JotDrop".
- Backward-compat: notes with old `<!-- obsidrop-preview -->` or `<!-- diexar-preview -->` markers still resolve via the plugin side.

And the voice-memo round (v0.20.x):

- One-tap recorder FAB on the dashboard (.m4a, MediaRecorder).
- Inline audio player + duration counter in the editor.
- Equalizer-banner on voice-memo cards.
- Refcount-aware delete for shared OG-thumbnails and orphan audio cleanup.

## Install

1. Download `jotdrop-debug.apk` below.
2. Install over the existing app (same signature, no uninstall needed).

The matching Obsidian plugin update is on tag [`0.14.1`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/releases/tag/0.14.1).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/compare/v0.21.0...v0.21.1
releases.name: JotDrop Android v0.21.1 — minor fixes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Renamed: ObsiDrop → JotDrop

Obsidian's automated plugin review flagged the previous name "ObsiDrop" for using the Obsidian trademark, and the description for the redundant "Obsidian" word. This release ships under the new name **JotDrop** ("jot + drop": quick-note + capture). Same app, same features — just rebranded.

**Installing over the previous APK:** the package id is unchanged (`com.diexar.keepcapture`) and the debug-keystore is the same, so this APK installs directly over `v0.20.x` without uninstalling. Your vault folder, captured notes, settings, attachments, and voice memos all stay put.

## What carries over from the previous APK

This release rolls up everything since v0.19.0, including:

- **Voice memos** — microphone FAB above the camera button on the dashboard. One tap starts (pulses red, switches to a stop icon), one tap ends. Confirmation dialog with duration. Saved as `.m4a` attachments embedded in a new card. Inline audio-player in the editor (play/pause + position counter). Equalizer-banner on voice-memo cards.
- **Refcount-aware delete** — single and bulk delete now scan Mini Notes + Archive before removing embedded images **or** audio. Shared OG-thumbnails stay safe. Fixes a slow data-bloat issue where `.attachments/` filled up.
- **Earlier v0.19.x fixes:** PNG validation in OgFetcher (Content-Type sniffing), FlowRow tag chips (no wrap-inside-chip in narrow card columns), "Save article images" toggle in settings.

## Backward compatibility

- Notes with the old `<!-- obsidrop-preview -->` or `<!-- diexar-preview -->` pending markers still get rescued by the plugin side.
- Android package id is unchanged (`com.diexar.keepcapture`), so this is a drop-in update — Android's installer accepts the same signature.

## Install

1. Download `jotdrop-debug.apk` below.
2. Open on your phone — Android prompts for "install from unknown sources" → grant.
3. If you had ObsiDrop (≤ v0.20.x) or Diexar Keep installed: this APK installs over it cleanly. Your vault selection persists.
4. The voice-memo recorder needs `RECORD_AUDIO`; first tap will ask permission.

The matching Obsidian plugin update is on tag [`0.14.0`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/releases/tag/0.14.0).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/compare/v0.20.2...v0.21.0
releases.name: JotDrop Android v0.21.0 — renamed from ObsiDrop

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Voice memos** — new microphone FAB above the camera button on the dashboard. One tap starts recording (FAB pulses red, icon switches to stop), one tap ends and shows a confirmation dialog "Voice memo of M:SS — save?". Cancel discards; Save creates a new card with the audio embedded as `.m4a`.
- **Inline audio player in the editor** — voice-memo cards now show a play/pause tile at the top of the editor with a duration counter. Shape-based state (play ↔ pause) for colourblind accessibility.
- **Equalizer-banner on voice-memo cards** — kaartjes met audio krijgen een herkenbare equalizer-banner in plaats van een lege ruimte, zodat je in één oogopslag ziet dat het om een voicememo gaat.
- **Refcount-aware deletion** — when you delete a card (single or bulk), its embedded image and/or audio is removed from `.attachments/` too — but only when no other card (incl. Archive) still references it. Shared OG-thumbnails between different link-cards stay safe. Fixes a subtle data-bloat bug where `.attachments/` filled up over time.
- **Bug fix:** `findEmbeddedImageBasenames` now filters Obsidian-style `![[...]]` embeds on extension, so a `.m4a` no longer claims the thumbnail slot and leaves an empty 16:9 area on the card.
- **Earlier v0.19.x fixes included in this APK:** PNG validation in OgFetcher (Content-Type sniffing), FlowRow tag chips (no more wrapping inside narrow card columns), and a "Save article images" toggle in settings.

## Install

1. Download `obsidrop-debug.apk` below.
2. Open on your phone — Android prompts for "install from unknown sources" → grant.
3. If you already had ObsiDrop installed, this installs over it (same debug-keystore, no uninstall needed). Vault folder stays selected.
4. The voice-memo recorder needs `RECORD_AUDIO`; first tap will ask permission.

The matching Obsidian plugin update is on tag [`0.13.0`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/releases/tag/0.13.0).

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/compare/0.12.0...v0.20.2
releases.name: ObsiDrop Android v0.20.2 — voice memos

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
Cosmetic-only patch om de Obsidian community-plugins review-warnings op te ruimen. Geen feature- of gedragswijzigingen.

## Fixes
- **styles.css**: duplicate `.jotdrop-card` selector samengevoegd in één rule
- **styles.css**: 3-digit hex-kleuren naar 6-digit format (`#d33` → `#dd3333`, `#fff` → `#ffffff`, `#111` → `#111111`)
- **styles.css**: `!important` op `.jotdrop-support-button` weggehaald — vervangen door selector-specificity (`.jotdrop-support-buttons a.jotdrop-support-button`)
- **esbuild.config.mjs**: `builtin-modules` npm-dep vervangen door Node's native `import { builtinModules } from "module"`, inclusief `node:`-prefixed varianten in externals
- **package.json**: `builtin-modules` devDependency verwijderd

## Wat blijft hetzelfde
Alle features van 0.14.1 (voicememo's, audio-player, refcount-aware delete, kaart-grid, search/tag-filter, reminders, embed-handling) werken ongewijzigd.
releases.name: JotDrop 0.14.2 — review-warnings opgeruimd

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
## What's new

Minor patch on top of 0.14.0. No user-facing UI changes — the display name remains **JotDrop** (one word).

If you installed via Community plugins, the listing will update automatically. Manual install: replace `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` in `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/jotdrop/`.

Matching Android APK: [`v0.21.1`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/releases/tag/v0.21.1).
releases.name: JotDrop 0.14.1 — minor fixes

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
## Renamed: ObsiDrop → JotDrop

Obsidian's automated plugin review flagged the previous name as using the Obsidian trademark and the description for the redundant "Obsidian" word. This release ships the plugin under the new name **JotDrop** ("jot + drop": quick-note + capture). Same plugin, same features — just rebranded.

If you installed via **Community plugins** in Obsidian: the listing will update once this version propagates. If you installed manually: download the new `main.js` / `manifest.json` / `styles.css` below, drop them in `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/jotdrop/`, and remove the old `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/` folder if you still have it.

## What carries over from the previous release

This release rolls up everything since 0.12.0, including:

- **Voice memos** — one-tap recorder in the toolbar (webm/opus), inline `<audio>` player in the edit modal, equalizer-banner on voice-memo cards.
- **Refcount-aware delete** — single and bulk delete now scan the whole vault (Mini Notes + Archive) before removing embedded images **or** audio from `.attachments/`. Shared OG-thumbnails stay safe.
- **Bug fix** for Obsidian-style `![[…]]` embeds: extension-filtered, so a `.m4a` no longer reserves the image-thumbnail slot on a card.

## Backward compatibility

- Old `<!-- obsidrop-preview -->` and `<!-- diexar-preview -->` pending markers are still recognized — notes from older Android builds keep resolving.
- `obsidian://obsidrop-clip` clip-URI still works alongside the new `obsidian://jotdrop-clip`, so Chrome extensions installed before this rename don't break until they auto-update.

## Install

**Via Community plugins** (after listing updates):
1. Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search **JotDrop** → Install → Enable.

**Manual**:
1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css` from this release.
2. Place in `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/jotdrop/` (create the folder).
3. Reload Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → enable **JotDrop**.

The matching Android APK is on tag [`v0.21.0`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/jotdrop/releases/tag/v0.21.0).
releases.name: JotDrop 0.14.0 — renamed from ObsiDrop

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
## Highlights

- **Voice memos** — one-tap recorder on the ObsiDrop dashboard. Records to `.webm`/opus (plays cross-device with the Android `.m4a` memos over Syncthing). Confirmation modal asks before saving so accidental taps don't clutter the vault.
- **Audio playback in the edit modal** — voice-memo cards now show a native `<audio>` player at the top, alongside the existing image-thumbnail flow.
- **Equalizer-banner on voice-memo cards** — visually recognizable at a glance, no audio plays until you open the note.
- **Refcount-aware delete** — when you delete a card (single or bulk), embedded images **and** audio go to the OS recycle bin too, but only when no other card or archived note still references that attachment. Shared OG-thumbnails stay safe.
- **Bug fix**: Obsidian-style `![[…]]` embeds are now extension-filtered. Previously a `.m4a` embed could falsely claim the thumbnail slot and leave an empty reserved area on the card.

## Install

Drop `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/` and reload Obsidian. Or wait for the community-plugins update if you installed from there.

The Android companion APK for the same release window is on the [`v0.20.2`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/releases/tag/v0.20.2) tag.
releases.name: ObsiDrop 0.13.0 — voice memos

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description:
## What's new in v0.12.0

- **Due-date reminders** — per-note reminders with notifications, survive reboot
- **Image lightbox** — tap a thumbnail to open full-screen; falls back to external open on desktop
- **Chrome web clipper** — optional HTTP clip-server so the companion extension can drop pages directly into your vault
- **Search & filter chips** — filter by title, body, or tag; top-N tag chips with a "+more" sheet
- **Bulk select** — long-press to enter selection mode; bulk archive or delete with confirmation

## Assets
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `main.js` | Compiled Obsidian plugin bundle |
| `manifest.json` | Plugin manifest (id: obsidrop, minAppVersion: 1.5.0) |
| `styles.css` | Plugin UI styles |

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/compare/v0.1.4...v0.12.0
releases.name: ObsiDrop v0.12.0 — reminders, lightbox, clip-server, search/filter chips, bulk-select

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description: See v0.12.0 for full release notes.
releases.name: ObsiDrop 0.12.0 — reminders, lightbox, clip-server, search/filter chips, bulk-select

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description: The biggest release since v0.1.0. Reminders, a Chrome web-clipper, tag-filter chips, bulk-select, and a full lightbox for image embeds — on both Android and Obsidian. ## What's new ### Reminders (Android + plugin) - Per-note due-date with picker (date + time-input, no fiddly MIUI clock-hell). - Android notification fires even when the app is closed; uses `AlarmManager.setAndAllowWhileIdle`. - "⏰ Due" / "⚠ Overdue" badge right on the card. Shape + label, not just color (colorblind-safe). - Reminders survive reboot (`BootReceiver` reschedules them). ### Search + tag-filter chips - Search bar above the grid (title + body + tags, substring, case-insensitive). - **Tag-filter chip strip** under the search bar — top-N by frequency, plus a "+N more" overflow sheet with a searchable list of every tag in your vault. - Selected chips get an explicit ✓ icon (not just an accent color). - AND between search × tags; OR within selected tags. ### Bulk-select - **Long-press** a card to enter selection mode — that card gets selected, tap any other card to toggle. - Top-bar swaps into × / "N selected" / ✓✓ select-all / 📦 archive / 🗑 delete. - Selection visual: 2px outline + filled-vs-empty circle marker (shape, not color). - Bulk archive / delete with confirmation. Sequential under the hood (SAF / vault operations aren't guaranteed thread-safe). - Escape (plugin) and the system Back button (Android) leave selection mode. ### Lightbox for image embeds - Tap a card thumbnail → full-screen viewer with "Open externally" fallback. - Works in both Obsidian and Android. Embeds are kept on save instead of being stripped. ### Chrome extension (new!) - Companion **web clipper** — one click sends the current page (URL + title + selection + tags) into your vault as a Markdown note. - Talks only to a localhost server inside the plugin (`127.0.0.1`, token-authenticated, off by default). - Tag input is chip-based: type a tag, hit Enter, chip appears. Backspace on an empty input removes the last chip. ### Quality of life - Sunset / dusk theme on Android — gradient surfaces, 16dp rounded corners, 2-column portrait / 4-column landscape grid (≥600dp). - OCR on photos via ML Kit Text Recognition v2 (Latin script bundled, ~3-4MB — works without Google Play Services). - Voice-to-text on Android via system speech recognizer. - Inline `- [ ]` checklist toolbar in both editors. - Embeds shown as thumbnails in the editor's body field (not as raw `![[...]]` markdown). - **Hard tag isolation** — tags only ever come from the dedicated tag input. The title is stripped of `#tag` syntax; the body escapes `#tag` to `\#tag` so it never pollutes the Obsidian graph view. ## Component versions - Plugin: **0.12.0** - Android: **0.19.0** (versionCode 39) - Chrome extension: **0.1.2** ## Install **Obsidian plugin** (desktop + mobile) — download `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css` → drop in `/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/`, enable in Settings → Community plugins. **Android app** — install `obsidrop-debug.apk`. Updates from v0.1.3 install in-place (same signing key). **Chrome extension** — clone or download the [`chrome-extension/`](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/tree/main/chrome-extension) folder, load it unpacked at `chrome://extensions` (Developer mode), and paste the token from the plugin's settings. See the [README](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop#readme) for screenshots and the full setup walk-through. **Full Changelog**: https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop/compare/v0.1.3...v0.1.4
releases.name: ObsiDrop v0.1.4 — reminders, web clipper, search/filter & bulk-select

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Tooling fix: APK updates now install over the previous version without an uninstall step. From this release on, the "App not installed" prompt should be a thing of the past. ## What's new - **Stable APK signing** - the repo now ships a fixed debug keystore that CI uses for every build, so the signature is identical across releases. Android no longer treats the new APK as a different app. ## One-time upgrade step Because v0.1.2 was signed with the old per-CI-run key, **this update still requires one uninstall**: 1. Uninstall ObsiDrop on your phone 2. Install `obsidrop-debug.apk` from this release 3. Reopen ObsiDrop and re-grant the notes-folder permission (your notes themselves stay safe in the folder) From v0.1.4 onwards updates will install in-place. ## Component versions - Plugin: 0.10.1 (unchanged from v0.1.2) - Android: 0.15.3 (versionCode 29) ## Download **Obsidian plugin** (works on desktop + mobile) - `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css` -> `/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/`, then enable in Settings → Community plugins. **Android app** - `obsidrop-debug.apk` (see "One-time upgrade step" above). See [README](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop#readme) for full install instructions.
releases.name: v0.1.3

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Cards with link previews now actually pick up a thumbnail on a much wider range of sites. Previously, plenty of legitimate sites returned a blank card because either (a) their server blocked our scraper or (b) their `og:image` pointed to a 404. ## What's new - **Browser-fingerprint headers** - we now send the full Chrome `sec-ch-ua` / `sec-fetch-*` set, so Cloudflare/WAF-fronted sites stop classifying us as a bot. Same fix is applied to both HTML scrapes and image downloads. - **Image fallback chain** - if `og:image` returns 404 (more common than you'd think), the plugin/app automatically tries `twitter:image`, `og:image:secure_url`, JSON-LD `image`, `apple-touch-icon`, and as a last resort a same-site `` from the page body. Third-party widgets (Google login buttons, FB pixels) are filtered out by domain match. - **Same improvements on Android** - the share-flow on your phone now picks up previews just as reliably as the desktop plugin. ## Component versions - Plugin: 0.10.1 (OG-preview improvements) - Android: 0.15.2 (versionCode 28) ## Download **Obsidian plugin** (works on desktop + mobile) - `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css` -> `/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/`, then enable in Settings → Community plugins. **Android app** - `obsidrop-debug.apk` installs cleanly over v0.1.1 (versionCode bumped). See [README](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop#readme) for full install instructions.
releases.name: v0.1.2

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Small but noticeable polish release: a proper splash screen instead of a 2-second black void on cold-start. ## What's new - **Splash screen on cold-start** - deep-aubergine background with the app icon, then it hands off to the regular gradient theme. The actual load time is unchanged, but the wait now *feels* instant because you see something polished immediately. ## Component versions - Plugin: 0.10.0 (unchanged from v0.1.0) - Android: 0.15.1 (versionCode 27) ## Download **Obsidian plugin** (works on desktop + mobile) - `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css` -> `/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/`, then enable in Settings → Community plugins. **Android app** - `obsidrop-debug.apk` installs cleanly over v0.1.0 (versionCode bumped). See [README](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop#readme) for full install instructions.
releases.name: ObsiDrop v0.1.1 - splash screen on cold-start

releases.author: Diexar-Labs
releases.description: First public release of ObsiDrop - a Google Keep-style quick-capture for [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/) with a matching Android share-target app. ## Download **Obsidian plugin** (works on desktop + mobile) - grab `manifest.json`, `main.js`, `styles.css` from the Assets below and drop them in `/.obsidian/plugins/obsidrop/`. Then enable in Settings → Community plugins. **Android app** - download `obsidrop-debug.apk` and install on your phone (you'll need to allow installs from this source). The APK is debug-signed so future updates install over the previous version cleanly. ## What's inside - Card-grid view in Obsidian (Keep-style, 2-col mobile, multi-col desktop) - Android share-target - share any link/text/image from any app into your vault - OCR (ML Kit Text Recognition v2 - bundled, no Google Play needed) - Voice-to-text on Android - Open Graph link previews - Inline `- [ ]` checklists with smart toolbar toggle - Colors, tags, archive, pinned - Plain Markdown files with YAML frontmatter - designed to coexist with Syncthing ## Component versions - Plugin: 0.10.0 - Android: 0.15.0 (versionCode 26) ## Languages EN (default), NL. Skeletons for ES/DE/FR/IT - contributions welcome. ## Known limitations - Plugin not yet in Obsidian community-plugins store (manual install for now) - APK is debug-signed - fine for personal use, not Play Store quality - iOS share-target not yet supported See [README](https://github.com/Diexar-Labs/obsidrop#readme) for full install + setup instructions.
releases.name: ObsiDrop v0.1.0 — first public release
